Name: Biodefense & Health Systems
Description: Technologies protecting human health through decentralized pathogen surveillance, open-source medical devices, and resilient healthcare infrastructure.
Key Implementations: Varro Inc., Pathoplexus, OSLUV, Openwater, MediLedger
Key Institutional Players: Balvi, Gates Foundation, BARDA, DARPA
Regulatory Context: FDA medical device pathways, CDC surveillance, WHO pandemic preparedness
Required Primitives: Secure data sharing, privacy-preserving computation, decentralized storage

Name: Open Source Hardware & Silicon
Description: Open-source hardware, silicon, and mobile operating systems reducing dependence on centralized manufacturers.
Key Implementations: GrapheneOS, Freedom Factory (ethOS), Nanographs, RISC-V
Key Institutional Players: RISC-V Foundation, Linux Foundation, Mozilla, Open Compute
Regulatory Context: Export controls on semiconductors, open source licensing
Required Primitives: Formal verification, secure boot, hardware attestation

Name: Resilient Manufacturing
Description: Decentralized, open-source manufacturing including robotics, 3D printing, and assistive technology.
Key Implementations: k-scale, SalvageGarden, Simula, FarmHack, Open Source Ecology
Key Institutional Players: Fab Foundation, Maker Movement, RepRap Project
Regulatory Context: Product safety standards, manufacturing compliance
Required Primitives: CAD/CAM sharing protocols, supply coordination

Name: Decentralized Energy
Description: Peer-to-peer energy trading, decentralized grid management, and renewable energy coordination.
Key Implementations: Power Ledger, Energy Web Foundation, LO3 Energy
Key Institutional Players: Energy Web Foundation, Rocky Mountain Institute, IEA
Regulatory Context: Utility regulations, net metering, grid interconnection
Required Primitives: Oracles for energy data, smart contracts, IoT

Name: Property Rights & Registries
Description: Blockchain-based land registries, property tokenization, and decentralized title systems.
Key Implementations: Georgia Land Registry, Dubai Property Platform, Rwanda Cadastre, RealT
Key Institutional Players: World Bank, UN-Habitat, national land agencies
Regulatory Context: Government partnerships required, property law varies
Required Primitives: Identity verification, secure storage, government APIs

Name: Carbon & Environmental Markets
Description: On-chain carbon credits, environmental tokenization, and decentralized climate markets.
Key Implementations: Toucan Protocol, KlimaDAO, Verra
Key Institutional Players: Gold Standard, Verra, Climate Action Reserve
Regulatory Context: Voluntary carbon standards, Paris Agreement Article 6
Required Primitives: Oracles, tokenization standards, MRV systems

Name: Civic Tech
Description: Community-driven technology for civic engagement, urban farming, maker spaces, and local coordination.
Key Implementations: Better.SG, Edible Garden City, Soil Social, Chiang Mai Maker Club
Key Institutional Players: Code for America, mySociety, Civic Tech communities
Regulatory Context: Generally light-touch regulation
Required Primitives: Local coordination tools, community governance

Name: Zero-Knowledge Systems
Description: Zero-knowledge proof infrastructure enabling verification without revealing underlying data. Core d/acc primitive.
Key Implementations: Aztec Network, zkSync, StarkNet, Polygon, Scroll, Taceo
Key Institutional Players: Ethereum Foundation, a16z, Paradigm, Protocol Labs
Regulatory Context: Privacy regulations (GDPR) may favor ZK, sanctions discussions
Required Primitives: Cryptographic primitives, proving systems, verifiers

Name: Privacy-Preserving Computation
Description: FHE, MPC, and secure enclaves enabling computation on encrypted data without decryption.
Key Implementations: Zama (FHE), ZenGo (MPC), Fireblocks, Inco Network
Key Institutional Players: DARPA, Microsoft Research, Intel SGX, a16z
Regulatory Context: GDPR compliance, healthcare privacy, financial confidentiality
Required Primitives: FHE libraries, MPC protocols, TEEs

Name: Decentralized Identity & Attestation
Description: Self-sovereign identity, verifiable credentials, and attestation systems without centralized authorities.
Key Implementations: ENS, Worldcoin, Polygon ID, EAS, Gitcoin Passport, QuarkID
Key Institutional Players: W3C, DIF, Ethereum Foundation, governments
Regulatory Context: eIDAS 2.0 in EU, digital ID frameworks emerging
Required Primitives: DID standards, verifiable credentials, ZK attestations

Name: Communication & Messaging
Description: End-to-end encrypted, decentralized messaging and communication protocols.
Key Implementations: XMTP Labs, HOPR, Waku, Status, Session
Key Institutional Players: Signal Foundation, Protocol Labs, Status Network
Regulatory Context: Encryption debates, backdoor mandates in some jurisdictions
Required Primitives: E2E encryption, metadata protection, decentralized routing

Name: Secrets-as-a-Service
Description: Programmable access control and secrets management. Determines WHO can access WHAT data WHEN.
Key Implementations: Mysten Labs (Sui), Lit Protocol, Privy
Key Institutional Players: a16z, Paradigm, HashiCorp
Regulatory Context: Key management compliance, enterprise security standards
Required Primitives: Threshold cryptography, access control, key derivation

Name: Formal Verification & Security
Description: Smart contract auditing, formal verification, bug bounties, and security tooling.
Key Implementations: Trail of Bits, OpenZeppelin, Spearbit, Immunefi, Code4rena
Key Institutional Players: Ethereum Foundation, major protocols, security researchers
Regulatory Context: Emerging audit standards, liability frameworks developing
Required Primitives: Formal verification tools, static analysis, runtime monitoring

Name: Democratic Funding Mechanisms
Description: Quadratic funding, retroactive public goods funding, and novel resource allocation mechanisms.
Key Implementations: Gitcoin, Optimism RetroPGF, Octant, Hypercerts
Key Institutional Players: Ethereum Foundation, Optimism Collective, Protocol Labs
Regulatory Context: Grant-making frameworks, charitable giving regulations
Required Primitives: Sybil resistance, quadratic voting, impact measurement

Name: Epistemic Infrastructure
Description: Prediction markets, fact-checking systems, and tools for improving collective epistemics.
Key Implementations: Metaculus, Polymarket, CheckMateSG, Deepsafe
Key Institutional Players: Good Judgment Project, forecasting community
Regulatory Context: Gambling regulations apply to prediction markets
Required Primitives: Outcome oracles, reputation systems, information aggregation

Name: Governance Tooling
Description: DAO governance frameworks, voting systems, and collective decision-making tools.
Key Implementations: Safe, Argent, Aragon, Compound Governor, Tally, Snapshot
Key Institutional Players: Major DAOs, Ethereum Foundation, a16z
Regulatory Context: DAO legal frameworks emerging, securities considerations
Required Primitives: Multisig, voting mechanisms, delegation, timelock

Name: Decentralized Monetary Infrastructure
Description: Decentralized stablecoins, UBI protocols, and monetary systems without central bank control.
Key Implementations: DAI (MakerDAO), Circles/CirclesUBI, Ethena (USDe)
Key Institutional Players: MakerDAO, Ethereum Foundation, central banks
Regulatory Context: Stablecoin regulations emerging, MiCA in EU
Required Primitives: Collateral management, oracles, stability mechanisms

Name: Oracle Networks
Description: Decentralized data feeds connecting blockchains to real-world information. Critical infrastructure.
Key Implementations: Pyth, Chronicle, RedStone, Chainlink
Key Institutional Players: Major DeFi protocols, data providers
Regulatory Context: Data quality standards, liability frameworks developing
Required Primitives: Data aggregation, stake-based security, latency optimization

Name: Cross-Chain Infrastructure
Description: Cross-chain messaging and bridging enabling interoperability between blockchains.
Key Implementations: LayerZero, Axelar, Wormhole, CoW Protocol, UniswapX
Key Institutional Players: Bridge protocols, DEXs, major chains
Regulatory Context: Bridge security standards emerging, exploit liability
Required Primitives: Message passing, proof verification, liquidity management

Name: Data Availability & Storage
Description: Modular data availability layers and decentralized storage for blockchain scalability.
Key Implementations: Celestia, EigenDA, Avail, Filecoin, Arweave, The Graph
Key Institutional Players: Ethereum Foundation, Protocol Labs, modular chains
Regulatory Context: Data retention regulations, storage compliance
Required Primitives: Data sampling, erasure coding, proof systems

Name: Streaming & Treasury
Description: Token streaming protocols and programmable treasury management for continuous payments.
Key Implementations: Sablier, Superfluid, Llamapay
Key Institutional Players: DeFi protocols, DAOs, payroll companies
Regulatory Context: Payroll regulations, tax implications for streaming
Required Primitives: Payment channels, flow rates, composable streams
